source: Incus Server - 9 Snapshot Server: Copying snapshots across, boot.autostart, and the built-in snapshot scheduler

questions.q: How do you migrate a snapshot of rockylinux-test-9 to the snapshot server?
Before starting a migrated container on the snapshot server, what must you do?
options: Stop it on the primary first, to avoid an IP address conflict
questions.why: Both running at once would clash on the same IP, so stop it on the primary first.

questions.q: Why set `incus config set <name> boot.autostart 0` on migrated containers?
options: So they do not auto-start on a snapshot-server reboot and cause IP conflicts with the primary
questions.why: 0 disables autostart, preventing the snapshot copies from coming up and clashing after a reboot.

questions.q: How do you schedule automatic snapshots of a container (cron-like)?
questions.why: snapshots.schedule takes a cron-style timestamp (here 20:50 daily).

options: A meaningful (date/time-stamped) naming pattern for automatic snapshots
questions.why: snapshots.pattern sets the name template, e.g. embedding creation_date, since Incus uses UTC.

questions.q: What does `incus config set <name> snapshots.expiry 1d` do?
options: Automatically removes snapshots after they age past 1 day, so the disk does not fill up
Keeps snapshots for 1 day of work only
questions.why: snapshots.expiry prunes old snapshots so scheduled ones do not accumulate forever.
